A 12 cm circumferential diaphragmatic opening is performed.
After that, the diaphragm is retracted through the abdomen to get a full line visualization.
This section of apical adhesions was performed.
When the line is free, a complete line digital perturbation is performed.
This is to see if there are other lesions.
After finding the lesion, 60mm endosteplars and blue loads are used to perform a wedge resection of the lesion.
Pathological confirmation of the metastasis and safe margins was performed.
After that, a 24 French chest strain was placed.
Finally, a Effibon 1-0 diaphragmatic suture was performed.
